slice: 10 PR3 — insert-exotica (Insert tab) on the PM engine
reference: Word for Windows 16.0 (COM oracle scripts/oracle/word-oracle-win.ps1, PID-safe, foreground/STA, unsandboxed)

bisect.note: First 'all' run FAILED Word open (EXIT=1). Bisected to isolate the culprit.
bisect.cover: EXIT=1 (Word REFUSED to open) — root cause

rootCause: coverPage documentPartObject id was a non-numeric string ('wc-cover-837400'). The sdt <w:id w:val> is an ST_DecimalNumber; a non-numeric value is a hard schema violation (Word will not open the file). The exporter's sanitizeId keeps any non-empty string but drops a JS number, so the fix is a digits-only string (String(Date.now() % 1000000)) — survives sanitizeId AND validates.

legA.result: EXIT=0 — Word opened the full 5-construct doc WITHOUT repair and resaved it (17161 bytes).
legA.notes: Word canonicalized the WordArt run color (w14:textFill re-expressed elsewhere) and inlined the wps Choice without mc:AlternateContent — both normal Word serialization; the shapes/warp survived.

legB.result: openDocx=true, importBlanked=false — clone imports Word's own serialization HEALTHY.
legB.notes: Cover re-imports as an EDITABLE documentPartObject; WordArt as an editable vectorShape; textBox + DATE arrive as passthroughInline (content + OOXML preserved, re-exports) — see deferrals (block-level VML-textbox import is a recorded fork follow-up).

verdict: BOTH LEGS PASS. dropCap/coverPage/dateTime/WordArt/textBox export real Word OOXML that opens without repair, survives Word's resave, and re-imports into the clone healthy. The bisect-found cover-id schema violation is fixed.
gates: PM 310/310, legacy 257/257 byte-identical, smoke 9/9 x2, roundtrip 27/0, docx 17/0.
